issues.title: CassandraVectorStore: inconsistent behaviors of CassandraVectorStore.builder
issues.body:
Spring AI version: 1.0.0.M8

This is a follow up of https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/3115 

Currently I have

```
@Configuration
public class MyConfig {

...

    @Bean
    public VectorStore defaultVectorStore(CqlSession cqlSession,
                                          EmbeddingModel embeddingModel,
                                          @Value(KEY_SPACE_ENV) String keyspace) {
        return CassandraVectorStore.builder(embeddingModel)
                .session(cqlSession)
                .keyspace(keyspace) // keyspace = "agentic_develop"
                .table(CassandraVectorStore.DEFAULT_TABLE_NAME)
                .partitionKeys(List.of(new SchemaColumn(FILE_ID_COLUMN, TEXT), new SchemaColumn(CHUNK_ID_COLUMN, TEXT)))
                .clusteringKeys(List.of(new SchemaColumn(TS, BIGINT)))
                .addMetadataColumns(new SchemaColumn(SCORE_COLUMN, DOUBLE))
                .contentColumnName(CONTENT_COLUMN)
                .embeddingColumnName(EMBEDDING_COLUMN)
                .documentIdTranslator(documentIdTranslator)
                .primaryKeyTranslator(primaryKeyTranslator)
                .disallowSchemaChanges(false)
                .batchingStrategy(new TokenCountBatchingStrategy())
                .build();
    }
}
```

and I will get an error of "Caused by: org.springframework.beans.BeanInstantiationException: Failed to instantiate [org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.cassandra.CassandraVectorStore]: Factory method 'vectorStore' threw exception with message: keyspace springframework does not exist"

Now the thing is, this error only occurs when I use 

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-vector-store-cassandra</artifactId>
        </dependency>
in pom.xml.

If I switch this to


        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-cassandra-store</artifactId>
        </dependency>,
the error will be no more.

comments.body:
tl;dr:

In my case,
using org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-cassandra-store, only one bean of VectorStore will be provided 
by MyConfig::defaultVectorStore (and everything is fine, no errors)

using org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-starter-vector-store-cassandra, two beans (I know not why, maybe some misconfiguration on my end) of VectorStore will be provided,
one by MyConfig::defaultVectorStore, 
the other by CassandraVectorStoreAutoConfiguration::vectorStore. 

The latter will use the default keyspace of "springframework", which I did not create in my Cassandra; and hence the error.

> [@ZYMCao](https://github.com/ZYMCao) In your custom configuration, can you make the `VectorStore` type more specific (`CassandraVectorStore`) as below?
> 
> ```
> @Bean
>   public CassandraVectorStore defaultVectorStore(CqlSession cqlSession,
>                                         EmbeddingModel embeddingModel,
>                                         @Value(KEY_SPACE_ENV) String keyspace) {
> ```
> 
> I tried that in a test, and it seems to work, whereas when I use the return type `VectorStore`, the `ConditionalOnMissingBean` does not work.

Yes.
changing
from ```@Bean public VectorStore defaultVectorStore(...) {}```
to ```@Bean public CassandraVectorStore defaultVectorStore(...) {}```
does solve the issue. 

Thank you @sobychacko so much!!
I went so far as excluding CassandraVectorStoreAutoConfiguration, but it all makes sense now.

I think this issue can be closed now.
